title: cerastium pumilum subsp. gussonei
intro: Cerastium pumilum subsp. gussonei, commonly known as the Grenada mouse-ear or rock-joint chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cerastium pumilum subsp. gussonei include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
